questions.question: What is the recommended first investment for most beginners?
options: A hot stock tip from social media
questions.explanation: A broad market index ETF (like VOO or VTI) provides instant diversification, low fees, and proven long-term returns — ideal for beginners.

questions.question: What happens if you miss just the 10 best trading days over 20 years?
questions.explanation: Missing the 10 best days over a 20-year period can cut your annualized returns from ~9.8% to ~5.6% — nearly half. This is why staying invested matters more than timing.

questions.question: A Roth IRA's main advantage is:
options: Higher returns than a regular account
questions.explanation: Roth IRA contributions are made with after-tax money, but all growth and withdrawals in retirement are completely tax-free.

questions.question: Before investing, you should have an emergency fund of:
questions.explanation: Financial advisors recommend 3-6 months of living expenses in a savings account before investing, so you never have to sell investments at a bad time to cover emergencies.
